sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
After the pit straight, brake very hard at the 100m board and shift down to 2nd gear. Hug the inside kurb but dont ride it too aggressively — it can destabilise the car. Traction on exit is crucial, especially when defending or attacking into turn two. Use all of the track on exit, but avoid running wide onto the artificial grass.

Turn two is a flat-out right kink that sets up the tight, banked turn three, known as the Hugenholtzbocht. Brake in a straight line, shift down to 2nd or 3rd gear, depending on the racing situation, and hug the low line through the banked corner. Stay off the high line — you will struggle for grip and exit speed. Apply throttle smoothly at the bottom of the bowl to get good traction without spinning your rear wheels.

Turn four is flat out in modern F1 cars. Stay tight to the left to prepare for turn five, applying a slight lift or light brake, depending on your setup. Turn six is flat out, as you make your way over to the left of the track in preparation for turn seven.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
When you enter turn 7, Scheivlak, brake gently and commit to the inside line early. Use the apex curb lightly and be ready to correct mid-corner. Keep the car balanced as the track drops away slightly on exit.

A fast left-hander that flows directly from turn seven. A slight lift or dab of brakes may be needed, depending on fuel load and tire grip. Use the apex kurb, but dont overdo it — it can throw off the track with relative ease. The exit is crucial for setting up the next medium-speed complex.

Brake into turn eight and drop to 4th gear. Hug the apex tightly and get back on throttle early to carry momentum into turn nine, a tighter left. Brake gently into nine, down to 3rd, and avoid riding the inside kurb too aggressively. Keep it tight on exit, as you will need a clean line for the short straight to maintain your momentum.

A short straight leads into the 90-degree right-hander. Brake hard and turn in early to touch the inside kerb. Keep your line clean, as you need to flow straight into the next chicane-like section.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
A slow, technical chicane. Brake in a straight line, down to 2nd gear, and be patient. Attack the inside curbs lightly — too much pressure will cause the car to bounce. Good rotation in turn eleven is key to getting the nose pointed forward for a clean turn twelve and maximising traction for the run toward the final corner.

A slight right-hand kink, often taken nearly flat. Use it to align the car for the critical final corner. Hug the left-hand side before diving into turn fourteen.

This is Zandvoorts signature steeply banked final corner. Approach at high speed and turn in with confidence. Its nearly flat in an F1 car with proper grip. Stick to the mid-to-low line on the banking for the best exit speed. Nail the throttle early to blast down the pit straight. DRS activation comes quickly after the exit, so maximise speed here to defend or attack into turn one.
